description_long: This is a large, rectangular apartment building with a reddish-brown brick facade and a flat, dark gray roof. The structure features two main wings connected by a recessed central section, creating an overall 'H' shape when viewed from above. The front facade is lined with evenly spaced, vertically aligned windows, each with a white frame, giving the building a sense of order and symmetry. The ground level includes a recessed entrance area, likely serving as the main access point for residents. The sides and rear of the building are more austere, with fewer windows and no visible decorative elements. The overall appearance is utilitarian, typical of mid- to late-20th-century urban residential architecture.
description: A large, brick apartment building with a flat roof, symmetrical window arrangement, and a recessed central entrance. The structure is utilitarian and rectangular, with two main wings forming an 'H' shape.
description_view_0: Side view showing one wing of the building with a vertical row of windows and the recessed central section partially visible.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the main facade with two wings, a central recessed entrance, and a symmetrical grid of windows.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the other wing with a vertical row of windows and the flat roof.
description_view_3: Rear view with plain brick walls and no visible windows or entrances, highlighting the building's utilitarian back side.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in a regular, symmetrical grid pattern, vertically aligned and evenly spaced across the facade, with each wing mirroring the other.
